questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q: A straight line is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>36</mn><mo> </mo></math>cm long. Points are to be marked on the line from both the ends so that the first point is at a distance of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo> </mo></math>cm from one end, the second point is at a distance of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo> </mo></math>cm from the first point, the third point is at a distance of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mo> </mo></math>cm from the second point, and so on. If the points at the ends are not counted, and the common points are counted as one, then what is the number of points?
questions.solution:
Upon analysing the distances and the points from the given question, we can form the following table:

From the first end      From the second end
Point    

Distance

(From 1st end)

 Point     

Distance

(From 2nd end)
First 1 cm First 35 cm
Second 3 cm Second 33 cm
Third 6 cm Third 30 cm
Fourth 10 cm Fourth 26 cm
Fifth 15 cm Fifth 21 cm
Sixth 21 cm Sixth 15 cm
Seventh 28 cm Seventh 8 cm
Eighth 36 cm Eighth 0 cm

The points at 0 cm and 36 cm are endpoints. Hence, these two points are excluded. 

The four points at 15 cm and 21 cm are common points. Hence, two of these are also excluded.

Therefore, the required number of points 
=16-4=12.

Hence, the correct answer is 12.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions - The following pie chart shows the expenditure (in percentage) of five companies P, Q. R. S and T in the year 2016.

Total Expenditure =Rs. 48 crores

What was the total expenditure (in Rs. crores) of the company Q. R and T together?
questions.solution:
According to the question

Total expense percentage of companies Q, R and T =22%+18%+20% =60%

∴ Required Expenditure=48×60100=Rs. 28.8 crores.

Hence, the total expense of companies Q, R and T=Rs. 28.8 crores.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q: The sum of present ages of 3 persons is 72 yrs. Seven years ago, their ages were in the ratio \(4: 6: 7\). What are their present ages in years?
questions.solution:
Given:

The sum of present ages of 3 persons \(=72\) years

Seven years ago, their ages were in the ratio \(4: 6: 7\)

Formula Used:

Sum of present ages \(=x+y+z\)

Ratio relation: \((x-7):(y-7):(z-7)=4: 6: 7\)

Calculation:

Let the ages of three persons seven years ago be \(4 \mathrm{k}, 6 \mathrm{k}\), and 7 k respectively. Then, their present ages are \((4 k+7),(6 k+7)\), and \((7 k+7)\).

According to the given condition:

\(\begin{aligned}

& (4 \mathrm{k}+7)+(6 \mathrm{k}+7)+(7 \mathrm{k}+7)=72 \\

& \Rightarrow 4 \mathrm{k}+6 \mathrm{k}+7 \mathrm{k}+21=72 \\

& \Rightarrow 17 \mathrm{k}+21=72 \\

& \Rightarrow 17 \mathrm{k}=72-21 \\

& \Rightarrow 17 \mathrm{k}=51 \\

& \Rightarrow \mathrm{k}=51 / 17 \\

& \Rightarrow \mathrm{k}=3

\end{aligned}\)

So, their present ages are:

\(4 \mathrm{k}+7=4 \times 3+7=12+7=19\) years

\(6 k+7=6 \times 3+7=18+7=25\) years

\(7 k+7=7 \times 3+7=21+7=28\) years

The present ages are 19, 25, and 28 years.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q: There are some cows, bulls, and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>45</mn></math> hens in a group. One caretaker looks after <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>15</mn></math> animals. The number of bulls is twice the number of cows. If the number of the head is less than the total number of feet by <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>186</mn></math> (including the caretaker), then how many caretakers are there?
questions.solution:
Let the number of cows = x

So, the number of bulls = 2x

Number of hens =45
Number of caretakers required  = x+2x+4515
Number of feet =  

(Number of feet of cow) × (Number of cows) + (Number of feet of bull × (Number of bulls) +(Number of feet of hen) × (Number of hens) +(Number of feet of caretaker) × (Number of caretakers) 

= 4x+8x+90+2 (x+2x+4515)
Number of head = (Number of head of cow) × (Number of cows) +(Number of head of bull × (Number of bulls) +(Number of head of hen) × (Number of hens) +(Number of head of caretaker) × (Number of caretakers) 

= x+2x+45+x+2x+4515

Since,  4x+8x+90+2x+2x+4515-x+2x+45+x+2x+4515=186
   9x+45+x+2x+4515=186

9x+x+2x+4515=141

138x=2115-45

x=2070138=15
          
Number of caretakers =x+2x+4515=15+2×15+4515=9015=6

Hence, the number of caretakers is 6.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi></math> is half as old as <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>B</mi><mo>.</mo><mo> </mo><mi>C</mi></math> is twice as old as <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>B</mi></math>. How many times is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>C</mi></math> as old as <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><mo>?</mo></math>
questions.solution:
According to the question,

A is half as old as B that means,

 12B=A ... ($1$)

C is twice as old as B,

C=2B ... ($2$)

Compare eq. ($1$) and ($2$), we get

2A=C2

4A=C

Hence, C is 4 times older than A.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q: A is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn></math> times as heavy as B. B is half the weight of C. C is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>5</mn></math> times as heavy as D. D is one fourth the weight of E. Who is the heaviest?
questions.solution:
According to the question,

A =4B 

B =C2

C =2.5×D

D  =14×E

A : B =4 : 1=20 : 5

B : C  =1 : 2=5 : 10

C : D =5 : 2=10 : 4

D : E  =1 : 4=4 : 16

So, 

A : B : C : D : E =20 :  5 :  10  :  4  :  16

From this ratio, it is clear that A is the heaviest.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q: The average of nine numbers is 60, that of the first five numbers is 55 and the next three is 65. The ninth number is 10 less than the tenth number. Then, tenth number is -
questions.solution:
Given:

Average of nine numbers \(=60\)

Average of first five numbers \(=55\) and average of next three numbers \(=65\)

Tenth number \(=\) Ninth number +10

Concept used:

Average \(=\) Total sum of all numbers \(/\) (Count of the numbers)

Calculation:

The sum of nine numbers \(=60 \times 9=540\)

The sum of the first five numbers \(=55 \times 5=275\)

The sum of the next three numbers \(=65 \times 3=195\)

Ninth number \(=(540-275-195)=(540-470)=70\)

\(\therefore\) Tenth number \(=70+10=80\)
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q: Estimates based on cluster samples are usually more reliable:
questions.solution: We use cluster sampling in market research. When we cannot get the proper information about the whole population, so we take a cluster for information, and it is very economical and practical.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q: Six boys A, B, C, D, E and F play a game of Cards. Each has a pack of 10 Cards. F borrows 2 Cards from A, and gives away 5 to C who in his teams gives 3 to B, while B gives 6 to D who passes on 1 to E. How many Cards D has?
questions.solution:
After observing all the conditions we get that,

A-10, B-10, C-10, D-10, E-10, F-10

F borrows 2 Cards from A, and gives away 5 to C

A-8, B-10, C-15, D-10, E-10, F-7

C gives 3 to B

A-8, B-13, C-12, D-10, E-10, F-7

B gives 6 to D who passes on 1 to E

A-8, B-7, C-12, D-15, E-11, F-7

The number of cards with D is 15.

Hence option (D) is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
DIRECTIONS : Questions are based on the graphs given below in which trends in decadal growth of population of India and some selected states are shown from 1901 to 1981. The states can be grouped on the basis of the decadal growth rates in relation to all-India average for each of the decades from 1992-11 to 1992-2002.
Now among the choices given below each of the questions you have to choose one which suitably belongs or does not belong to the group mentioned in the question.

The state showing growth rate in four decades above all-India growth rate is
questions.solution:
According to the information from the graph,
West Bengal is showing the highest growth rate among all India growth rates over four decades. Other states have comparatively lower growth rate over the decades.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q: When there is a big gap between the item at the two ends, which type of diagram is useful and meaningful:
questions.solution:
When there is a big gap between the item at the two ends, then the bar diagram is useful.
The different types of Bar diagrams are:

  • Vertical bar diagram
  • Horizontal bar diagram
  • Grouped bar diagram
  • Stacked bar diagram.

Hence, option B is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Read the following graph carefully and answer the question given below:
Ratio of import and export of a company in different years



If import and export are increased by 20% and 10% respectively in 2001, the resultant export became ₹50000. What was the import in that year?
questions.solution:
From the graph, it is clear that the ratio of import I and export E in the year 2001 is 6.5 i.e.

IE=6.5  -1

Increased export, E1=E+10100E=1110E

Increased import, I1=I+20100I=1210I

Increased export,  E1=₹50000

So,

1110E=50000E=50000×1011E=₹45454.55

Using the given ratio of import and export and the increased import we get,

1210I145454.55=6.5I1=6.5×45454.55×1012I1=₹246212.15

Hence, the required import =₹246212.15
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q: The average age of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>25</mn></math> sub-oridinates in an office is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>30</mn></math> years. If the age of Manager is added, the average increases to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>31</mn></math> years. What is the age of the Manager?
questions.solution:
As per the given information:
Age of Manager = New Average Age + (No. of sub-ordinates × Change in Average)

Therefore,
Age of Manager =31+(25×1)=56years.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q: The batting average for 27 innings of a cricket player is 47 runs. His highest score in an innings exceeds his lowest score by 157 runs. If these two innings are excluded, the average score of the remaining 25 innings is 42 runs. Find his highest score in an innings.
questions.solution:
Given:

The batting average for 27 innings of a cricket player is 47 runs.

His highest score exceeds his lowest score by 157 runs.

If these two innings are excluded, the average of the remaining 25 innings is 42 runs.

Formula used:

Average run \(=\) Total run in total innings/Total number of innings

Calculation:

Sum of runs for 27 innings of a cricket player \(=47 \times 27=1269\)

Sum of runs for 25 innings of a cricket player \(=42 \times 25=1050\)

Sum of remaining 2 innings \(=1269-1050=219\)

Let the minimum score be \(x\) and the maximum score be \(x+157\)

According to the question,

\(\begin{aligned}

& x+x+157=219 \\

& \Rightarrow 2 x=219-157 \\

& \Rightarrow 2 x=62 \\

& \Rightarrow x=31

\end{aligned}\)

So, highest score \(=157+31\)

\(\Rightarrow 188\)

\(\therefore\) His highest score in an innings is 188.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q: An elevator has a capacity of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>12</mn></math> adults or <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>20</mn></math> children. How many adults can board the elevator with <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>15</mn></math> children?<gwmw style="display:none;"></gwmw>
questions.solution:
As per the question :

Let the weight of one adult  =x

And the weight of one child  =y

Then, total capacity =12×x =20×y

⇒y=3x5 

Let A be the number of adults that board the elevator with 15 children.

According to the question : 

15y+Ax=12x
 
15×3x5+A×x = 12×x

⇒9x+Ax=12x

⇒9+A=12

⇒A=3

Hence, 3 adults can board the elevator with 15 children.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q: An enterprising businessman earns an income of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>₹</mo><mn>1</mn></math> on the first day of his business. On every subsequent day, he earns an income which is just double of that made on the previous day. On the <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mn>10</mn><mrow><mi>t</mi><mi>h</mi></mrow></msup></math>day of business, his income is:
questions.solution:
Given, income on the first day = ₹1

Again, income on the second day = ₹ 1 × 2 = ₹ 21

Now, income on the third day = ₹ 2 × ₹ 21

= ₹ 22  and so on.

Therefore, income on the nth day = ₹ 2n−1

So, income on the 10th day  =₹210-1 = ₹29   

Hence, ₹29 is the correct answer.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q: To succeed in a competition, Jermilin requires an average score of 75 in four exams. His score in the first three exams is 78, 74 and 70. How many scores should he get in the fourth test to succeed in the competition?
questions.solution:
Given:

Jermilin's scores in the first three exams: 78, 74, 70

The average score required in all four exams: 75

Let x be the score Jermilin needs to get in the fourth exam to achieve an average score of 75.

Formula:

Average score \(=\) (sum of all scores) \(/\) (number of scores)

Calculation:

We can use this formula to solve for x :

\(75=(78+74+70+x) / 4\)

Multiplying both sides by 4 :

\(300=222+x\)

Subtracting 222 from both sides:

\(x=78\)

Therefore, Jermilin needs to score 78 in the fourth exam to achieve an average score of 75 in all four exams.

\(\therefore\) Option 4 is the correct answer.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q: The average weight of P and his three friends is 55 kg. If P is 4 kg more than the average weight of his three friends, what is P's weight (in kg)?
questions.solution:
Given:

The average weight of \(P\) and his three friends \(=55 \mathrm{~kg}\)

The weight of \(P=4 \mathrm{~kg}\) more than the average weight of his three friends

Formula used:

The total sum of the terms \(=\) Average \(\times\) Number of terms

Calculation:

The total weight of \(P\) and his three friends \(=55 \times 4=220 \mathrm{~kg}\)

Let, the average weight of three friends \(=x\)

So, the total weight of three friends \(=3 x\)

The weight of \(P=x+4\)

Then, \((x+4)+3 x=220\)

\(\Rightarrow 4 x+4=220\)

\(\Rightarrow 4 \mathrm{x}=220-4=216\)

\(\Rightarrow x=216 / 4=54\)

\(\therefore\) P's weight \(=4+54=58 \mathrm{~kg}\)

\(\therefore\) The P's weight (in kg) is 58 kg
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
The pie chart given below shows the expenditure (in percentage) of Mahesh. The monthly income of Mahesh is ₹26000.

If he invests 65% of his savings on purchasing gold, then how much amount (in ₹) does he spend on gold?
questions.solution:
Savings

= 26000 of 22%

=₹26000×22100=₹5720

Amount spend on purchasing gold:

₹5720×65100

=₹3718

So, Mahesh spends ₹3718 on purchasing gold.

Hence, the correct answer is ₹3718.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q: What is the value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>25</mn><mo>%</mo></math> of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mn>4</mn><mn>9</mn></mfrac></math>of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>5</mn></math> times of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>27</mn></math>?
questions.solution:
As given in the question, we simplify the question as:

25% of 49 of 5×27

Upon simplification, we get:

25% of 49 of 5×27

⇒25100×49×5×27⇒25100×60

⇒15
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
The following pie chart shows the expenditure (in percentage) of five companies P, Q, R, S and T in the year 2016.

Total Expenditure =Rs. 48 crores

By how much percent expenditure of company Q and R together is greater than that of company P?
questions.solution:
According to the question

Percentage of Q and R =(22+18)%=40%

∴ required percentage = 40-2424×100

=1624×100

= 2003 = 66.67%

Hence, the correct answer is 66.67 %.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q: Seven years from now, Anamika will be as old as Malini was 4 years ago. Srinidhi was born 2 years ago. The average age of Anamika, Malini and Srinidhi 10 years from now will be 33 years. What is the present age of Anamika?
questions.solution:
Let the present age of Anamika be \(A\), Malini be \(M\) and that of Srinidhi be \(S\).

The, according to the question:

1) Seven years from now, Anamika will be as old as Malini was 4 years ago.

\(\begin{aligned}

& A+7=M-4 \\

& \Rightarrow M=A+11 \\

& S=2 \text { years }

\end{aligned}\)

And,

2) Srinidhi was born 2 years ago. The average age of Anamika, Malini and Srinidhi 10 years from now will be 33 years.

\(\begin{aligned}

& \frac{(A+10+M+10+S+10)}{3}=33 \\

& A+M+S+30=33 \times 3 \\

& A+M+S=99-30 \\

& A+M+S=69

\end{aligned}\)

Now, by substituting the above values,

\(\begin{aligned}

& A+(A+11)+2=69 \\

& 2 A+13=69 \\

& A=56 \div 2

\end{aligned}\)

\(\mathrm{A}=28 \text { years }\)

Hence, the present age of Anamika is \(28\) years is the correct answer.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
During the last four years a doctor has shown annual growth of his income as given below

Year 2010 2011 2012 2013
Income (in thousands) 10 12.5 17.5 25

What would be his income growth in 2014?
questions.solution:
According to the question,

In 2010, the income of doctor was 10 thousands.

In 2011, the income of doctor was 12.5 thousands.

So, the increment was 2.5 thousands.

Similarly,

10 + 2.5 = 12.5 thousands

12.5 + 5 = 17.5 thousands

17.5 + 7.5 = 25 thousands

25 + 10 = 35 thousands

Hence, the correct answer is 35 thousands.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn></math> years younger than <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>C</mi></math> but one year older than<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo> </mo><mi>D</mi></math>. <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>D</mi></math> is one year older than <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>B</mi></math> but <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn></math> years younger than <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>C</mi></math>.<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo> </mo><mi>C</mi></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>15</mn></math> years old. What is the age of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>B</mi></math>?
questions.solution:
According to the question,

A=C−3 .......... (i)

A=D+1  ..........(ii)

D=B+1  ..........(iii)

D=C−4  .......... (iv)

C=15 ................(v)

Substituting the value of C in equation (iv), we get,

D= 11

Substituting the value of D in equation (iii), we get,
B = 10

∴ B's age is 10 years.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Direction: This question is based on the table and diagram of population growth.

The decadal growth rate between 1922 and 1932 is:
questions.solution:
We know the formula of growth rate,
⇒Final-InitialInitial×100%
According to the table and diagram,
Growth Rate,
  ⇒Year 1932 - Year 1922Year 1922×100%⇒252093390-238396327238396327×100%⇒5.75%
Hence, option A is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q: Ages of father and son add up to 50. Six years back father's age was 6 more than thrice his son's age. What will be the father's age 6 years hence?
questions.solution:
Let the age of father be F and that of son be S .

\(\begin{aligned}

& F+S=50 \text { (Given) } \\

& S=50-F \quad ...(i)

\end{aligned}\)

Six years back father's age was 6 more than thrice his son's age.

According to problem:

\((F-6)=3(S-6)+6\) ...(ii)

Substituting the value of equation (i) in (ii), we get:

\(\begin{aligned}

& F-6=3(50-F-6)+6 \\

& \Rightarrow F-6=3(44-F)+6 \\

& \Rightarrow F-6=132-3 F+6 \\

& \Rightarrow F+3 F=132+6+6 \\

& \Rightarrow 4 F=144 \\

& \Rightarrow F=144 / 4 \\

& \Rightarrow F=36

\end{aligned}\)

So, father's age 6 years hence \(=(36+6)=42\)

Hence, ' 42 ' is the correct answer.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q: T is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>5</mn></math> years older than U. U is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>8</mn></math> years younger than V. V is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn></math> years older than W. W is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>9</mn></math> years younger than X. Which of the following have same age?
questions.solution:
Let the age of X be a years.

Then,

Age of W =a-9 years

Age of V =a-9+3=a-6 years

Age of U =a-6-8=a-14 years

Age of T =a-14+5=a-9 years

It is clear from above that the ages of W and T are the same.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q: Amita's father was 38 years old when she was born, while her mother was 36 years old when her brother was born who was four years younger than her. What is the difference between the ages of his parents?
questions.solution:
Given:

Amita's father was 38 years old when she was born.

Her mother was 36 years old when her brother was born.

Her brother was four years younger than her.

Calculation:

Let the present age of Amita be \(P\) years.

So,

The age of her father \(=(38+P)\) years

The age of her brother \(=(P-4)\) years

The age of her mother \(=36+(P-4)=(32+P)\) years

Now, the age difference between her parents \(=(38+P)-(32+P)=6\) years

\(\therefore\) The difference between the ages of her parents is 6 years.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q: Mr. Star was a consistent batsman throughout his career. He played 80 innings before he retired. Had he scored 10 runs in his last innings, his average would have been 50. However, in his last innings, he was dismissed for 2 runs. What was his average at the end of the 79th innings?
questions.solution:
It is given that had he scored 10 runs in the last innings, his average would have been 50 .

\(\begin{aligned}

& \Rightarrow \frac{\text { Total runs scored }+10}{80}=50 \\

& \Rightarrow R+10=80 \times 50 \\

& \Rightarrow R+10=4000 \\

& \Rightarrow R=4000-10=3990

\end{aligned}\)

When he scored 2 runs in the last innings, the total runs scored in the first 79 innings was:

\(\Rightarrow R_{79 \text { innings }}=3990-2=3988\)

Average after 79 innings:

\(\Rightarrow \text { Average }=\frac{\text { Total runs scored }}{\text { Total innings played }}=\frac{3988}{79}\)

On dividing:

\(\Rightarrow \text { Average }=49+\frac{74}{79}\)

\(\therefore\) The average at the end of the 79th innings is:

\(49 \frac{74}{79}\)
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Six boys A, B, C, D, E, and F play a game of Cards. Each has a pack of 10 Cards. F borrows 2 Cards from A, and gives away 5 to C, who gives 3 to B, while B gives 6 to D, who passes on 1 to E. How many Cards D has?

(A) 9               (B) 8

(C) 11            (D) 15
questions.solution: Total no. of cards with D = No. of cards he is having in the beginning + cards borrowed from someone else - cards passed on to someone else = 10 + 6 - 1  = 15
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.solution:

=Sum of all observationsNumber of observations

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q: Weight of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi></math> is twice that of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>B</mi></math>. Weight of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>B</mi></math> is half that of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>C</mi></math>. Weight of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>C</mi></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn></math> times that of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>E</mi></math>. Weight of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>E</mi></math> is half that of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>D</mi></math>. Who is the heaviest?
questions.solution:
Let the weight of D be 4x

Therefore, weight of E =D2=4x2=2x

Similarly, weight of C =3E=3×2x=6x

And, weight of B =C2=6x2=3x

Therefore, weight of A =2B=2×3x=6x

Thus, both A and C are the heaviest.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Study the given graph and answer the following questions.

An International agency surveyed the performance of cars.

The Points are given on the basis of the following features

The points earned by Hyundai on the basis of comfort are what percent more/less than the points earned by Siena on the basis of ride/handle?
questions.solution:
The points earned by Hyundai on the basis of comfort

= (650×14)100

=9100100

=91

The points earned by Siena on the basis of ride/handle

= (600×18)100

=10800100

=108

Now, the required percentage is

=(108-91)108×100

=17108×100

=15.74%
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q: A man wears socks of two colours - black and brown. He has altogether 20 brown socks in a drawer. Supposing he has to take out the socks in the dark, how many must he take out to be sure that he has a matching pair.
questions.solution:
It is given that he has socks of 2 colours only.

It is obvious that if he only takes out one sock then he can’t have a matching pair (since by definition a pair is 2 socks).

If he takes out 2 socks, he could select 2 that are the same colour, but he could also select one of each colour — which is not a matching pair. Therefore, 2 socks are not enough to guarantee that he will have a matching pair

If he takes out 3 socks then there are 2 scenarios — the first being that the first two socks he selects are matching. The second is that the first two socks he picks don’t match (without loss of generality we can assume the first is black and the second is brown) - in this case, the next sock he selects must make a pair, since if it is black it will make a pair with the first sock, and if it is brown it will make a pair with the second sock.

Thus, we have shown that 3 socks will guarantee a pair.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q: A, B, C, D and E play a game of cards. A says to B, “If you give me three cards, you will have as many as E has and if I give you three cards, you will have as many as D has.” A and B together have 10 cards more than what D and E together have. If B has two cards more than what C has and the total number of cards is 133, how many cards does B have?
questions.solution:
According to the given information,

B-3=E --------(1)
B+3=D -------(2)
A+B=D+E+10 -----(3)
B=C+2 --------(4)
A+B+C+D+E=133 ------(5)

Now, if we add equations (1) and (2), we get,
2B=E+D --------(6)

Substituting equation (6) in (3),

A+B=2B+10      
A=B+10 --------(7)

Substituting equations (4), (6) and (7), in equation (5), we get,

(B+10) + B + (B-2) + 2B=133

5B+8=133

5B=125

B=25

Therefore, B has 25 cards.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
A student creates a table to show the relationship between the side length of a square and its area.

Which of the graphs below best represents this relationship?
questions.solution:
According to the formula of square,

Area of square=a2 

Where a is equal to side length,

It means area of a square is directly proportional to its side length,

Area of square α (length)2

It means if the length is zero, the area will also be zero as the curve will be reprsented by, y=x2.

So the curve will start from the origin. Hence, the correct answer is option 2.

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
The bar graph given below shows the sales of books (in a thousand number) from six branches of a publishing company during two consecutive years 2000 and 2001.
Sales of Books (in a thousand numbers) from Six Branches - B1, B2, B3, B4, B5 and B6 of a publishing Company in 2000 and 2001.


Total sales of branches B1, B3 and B5 together for both the years (in thousand numbers) is ______.
questions.solution:
Total sales of branches B1=(80+105)=185

Total sales of branches B3=(95+110)=205

Total sales of branches B5=(75+95)=170
Total sales =185+205+170=560
Hence, the total sales is 560.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q: Find out the number of people who do not play any game?
questions.solution:
As given in the Venn diagram, 25 plays cricket, 22 plays tennis and 16 plays both.
The number of people who play only cricket is 25 - 16 = 9
The number of people who play only tennis is 22 - 16 = 6
So, people who do not play any game are,
⇒40 - (6 + 9 + 16)
⇒40 - 31
⇒9
Hence, option A is the correct answer.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q: The average age of a group of 25 boys is 17 years. If one boy leaves the group, the average age becomes 17.2 years. What is the age of the boy who left the group?
questions.solution:
Given:

The average age of a group of 25 boys is 17 years. If one boy leaves the group, the average age becomes 17.2 years. We need to find the age of the boy who left the group.

Solution:

Step 1: Calculate the total age of all 25 boys.

Average age of 25 boys \(=17\) years

Total age of 25 boys \(=25 \times 17=425\) years

Step 2: Calculate the total age of 24 boys after one leaves.

New average age of 24 boys \(=17.2\) years

Total age of 24 boys \(=24 \times 17.2=412.8\) years

Step 3: Find the age of the boy who left the group.

Age of the boy who left \(=\) Total age of 25 boys - Total age of 24 boys

Age of the boy who left \(=425-412.8=12.2\) years

Conclusion:

The age of the boy who left the group is \(12.2\) years.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
